meta.paid_product: Tenses trainer course with @mashassdf, for grades 5-11, A1-B1

idioms.phrase: have a head like a sieve
idioms.meaning_ru: голова как решето, ничего не помнить

idioms.example: He was on the verge of laughter during the serious meeting.
If your party gets out of hand, the neighbors will call the police.
I don't have much of a sweet tooth.

idioms.phrase: have one's head in the clouds

idioms.meaning_ru: вот это открытие! и так очевидно

grammar_rules.explanation: to be не ставится перед обычным смысловым глаголом
grammar_rules.example: I do my homework. (NOT: I am do my homework)
I have visited Japan. I have lost my keys. I have lived here for 5 years.

grammar_rules.explanation: few/a few - исчисляемые; little/a little - неисчисляемые. Без 'a' = негатив, с 'a' = ок.
grammar_rules.example: I have few friends (sad). I have a few friends (ok).

grammar_rules.explanation: Перед смысловым глаголом, после to be
grammar_rules.example: I often go to the gym. He is always happy.

grammar_rules.explanation: Учить устойчивые сочетания целиком, не переводить по словам
grammar_rules.example: take a picture (NOT do/make a picture); a quick shower (NOT a fast shower)

confusable_words.explanation: advice [ədˈvaɪs] = существительное (неисчисл!) совет. advise [ədˈvaɪz] = глагол советовать.
confusable_words.example: He gave me good advice. (NOT: an advice/advices)

pronunciation_tips.note: British смягчает/опускает, American произносит чётко (car, mother)
